It will bring together early career researchers from various disciplines to discuss and analyse the material aspects of exploration and travel in the early modern period. Description The Göttingen Spirit Summerschool 2017 will focus on the material culture of exploration and academic travel between 1700 and 1900. It will bring together early career researchers from various disciplines to discuss and analyse the material aspects of exploration and academic travel in the early modern period. The material culture of exploration and academic travel has received attention in historical research so far. Yet, the material objects that were brought back from travels and expeditions, as well as the material equipment used during these journeys, played an important role in the process of knowledge production. They were crucial for the dissemination of new knowledge and the establishment of academic networks. The Summerschool will address the following questions: - How did the material culture of exploration and academic travel develop between 1700

texts.full_text: he evolution of assortative mating in a sympatric population. Females mate with one of two types of polygynous males according to a male's display of one of two sex-limited, autosomal traits; these male traits may be associated with differential phenotypic mortalities. Through a Fisherian runaway process, female preferences and male traits can become associated in linkage disequilibrium, leading to patterns of assortative mating. Dispersing males, whose rate of movement is dependent on mating success, carry female preference genes with thethem as they move through space within a population. To analyze this situation mathematically and understand how these factors interplay in driving speciation events or changes over time: 1) Model Parameters: Define parameters such like initial allele frequencies of male traits, dispersal rate coefficients for males with varying levels mating success. These will be essential to simulate the process dynamics. 2)Simulation Setup and Iteration Processes: - Initiate a population simulation where both female preferences (phenotypes) & corresponding genetic variants are tracked. 3\) Assortative Mating Dynamics Analysis - Analyze how initial preference for certain male traits evolves with each generation. This includes understanding the correlation between fitness and phenotypic diversity, thus determining potential evolutionary pathways. 4)Speciation Events Prediction: - Investigate when these preferences & trait correlations become strong enough to drive a significant genetic divergence among subpopulations leading towards speciation. 5) Statistical Analysis of Results After numerous simulations with varied parameters are run, statistical methods like regression analysis can be used for testing the hypothesis that dispersal rate and mating success have an impact on preference genes' spread. texts.full_text: formance certificate is awarded by the School of Music to recognize those undergraduate music students with degree emphases other than music performance as well as musically-talented students who major in other disciplines. The performance certificate will allow gifted and highly motivated students to acquire additional credit, professorial interaction and guidance, and departmental distinction in music performance. Incoming freshman may demonstrate their fitness for the performance certificate during their qualifying or scholarship audition. Continuing students are eligible to enter this program by application to the coordinator of the appropriate area faculty and upon recommendation of the applied instructor after demonstrating satisfactory preparation through an audition or a series of evaluations. To earn the Undergraduate Performance Certificate, students must complete: - A minimum of 18 credits in music courses. - At least 9 credits at the upper-division level (300-level and above). - Successful completion of recital requirements as determined by the School of Music. This typically includes a solo or chamber music performance that meets specific program standards. The curriculum is designed to enhance technical skills, broaden musical knowledge, and deepen understanding through: - Applied lessons in primary instrument/voice. - Ensemble participation reflecting various genres (e.g., orchestra, band, choir). - Performance-related courses such as master classes, literature studies, and pedagogy workshops. This study evaluated the relationship between health, work environment, and retirement-related job lock in workers > or =55 with recent occupational injuries. Workers completed a mailed, self-report survey about their pre- and post-injury health and functioning, work environment, and retirement plans. Bivariate and multivariate analyses determined those factors associated with retirement-related job lock. Over half of the respondents wanted to retire but could not because they needed job-related income or benefits. Factors associated with retirement-related job lock were indicative of poorer health and mental function and dissatisfaction with the workplace social environment. texts.full_text: NEW YORK — Halloween weekend proved more trick than treat for Broadway, with the majority of shows dipping at the B.O. in Week 22 (Oct. 22-28). Heck, even estimates for the much-hyped “Young Frankenstein” — whose producers have decided not to report weekly sales — fell off by about $100,000 to $1.4 million. Including those unofficial “Frank” estimates, total Rialto cume was down by more than $450,000 for 29 shows on the boards. Some legiters attributed the slippage in part to the run-up to Halloween, increasingly a holiday that distracts from legit offerings. Among the officially reported numbers, the largest dips of the week were seen by “The Drowsy Chaperone” ($498,972), off about $105,000; “Les Miserables” ($455,038), sliding more than $80,000; and “The Color Purple” ($788,545), sliding by just under $75,000 due to the illness of the show’s star Fantasia Barrino. Buckoff some performances. On a brighter note for Broadway during Halloween weekend was "Come From Away," which saw an increase in attendance and revenue compared to previous weeks at approximately $1 million box office take. The musical, known for its heartwarming story of Newfoundlanders welcoming stranded passengers from 9/11 flights into their community, continued to resonate with audiences despite the spooky season. Other shows that managed steady or slightly improved numbers included "The Book of Mormon" and "Aladdin," suggesting a mix in audience preferences during this holiday period. The decline for many Broadway productions highlights how events like Halloween can impact theater attendance as potential viewers opt for more traditionally festive activities over live performances on weekends when costumes, candy, and haunted attractions are plentiful. Despite the overall dip, industry insiders remain optimistic about the long-term prospects of these shows, noting that audiences often return to their favorite musicals after holiday distractions. texts.full_text: timize recursion in Haskell by moving the recursive call into what's known as "tail position." In traditional tail-recursive functions, the last operation performed is either an immediate return or a recursive call. This allows for efficient memory usage because only one stack frame needs to be maintained during recursion, reducing overhead and improving performance. The initial code example: ```haskell triangle :: Int -> Int triangle 0 = 0 triangle x = x + triangle (x - 1) ``` Is not tail-recursive due to the addition operation (`+`) preceding the recursive call `triangle`. texts.full_text: 15–9, Chapman won three of his four matches. He beat Bob Lewis in first day's singles and then beat Hal Sutton twice on the final day, in both the foursomes and the singles. Professional career Chapman turned professional in late-1981 and won a European Tour card on his first visit to the Qualifying School. From 1982 to 2002 he finished in the top 100 on the European Order of Merit 19 out of the 21 years, with a best ranking of 17th in 1988. In his first season, 1982, he finished joint runner-up behind Sam Torrance in the Spanish Open. After six second-place finishes on the tour, he won the 2000 Brazil Rio de Janeiro 500 Years Open on his 472nd tour start. He also won the 1988 Zimbabwe Open on the Safari circuit and the Hassan II Golf Trophy in Morocco in 2000. He represented England in the 2000 Alfred Dunhill Cup. In May 2012, Chapman won the Senior PGA Championship, one of the five senior major championships for his maiden victory on the Champions Tour. After holding a five stroke lead entering the final round, he prevailed by two over American John Cook. He won his second senior major in July 2012 at the U.S. Senior Open. He won by two strokes over Fred Funk, Bernhard Langer, Tom Lehman, and Corey Pavin. In December 2018 he won the MCB Tour Championship – Seychelles on the European Senior Tour, beating Phillip Price with an eagle 3 at the first hole of a sudden-death playoff. Earlier Tom Lehman and Miguel Ángel Martín came to the final hole needing a par-5 to tie with Chapman and Price but took 6 and 8 respectively. King's body taken to royal residence to lie in state 12 September 2006 The body of the Tongan King Taufa'ahau Tupou IV is lying in state at his royal residence in Auckland but only after a dash through early-morning, peak-hour traffic. The king, who died late on Sunday night, was taken to a funeral home in Papakura, south Auckland yesterday, to be prepared forthe journey back across the Tasman. His body arrived by hearse just before 8am today at his official residence Villa Ta'unga Manuia near Otahuhu. The king's daughter Princess Pilolevu Tuita and her husband Lord Siosifa Fotofili were among a small group of family members who met him, along with about eight Tongan men dressed in traditional black clothing. They had earlier performed the kava ceremony at Auckland Airport after his body arrived from Nuku'alofa. His son Crown Prince Tupouto'a was expected to arrive later today but there is no word yet on when other royal family members will travel across for funeral ceremonies which are likely be held over several days in Tongatapu and Vavau, the two main islands of his Pacific

texts.full_text: English: “Sorry ladies, I have no idea what you are saying.” To which one responded, ”Puuucha… eres mas chileno que los porotos.” (“Damn, you’re more Chilean than beans.”) I was flattered—no one hearing my Spanish would think I was Chilean—and amused to hear this classic Chilean expression. Since beans have been Chilean and Chileans have been bean eaters for about 5,000 years[2], the saying has merit. When the Spanish entered Chile two bean species were being grown, porotos and pallares (respectively common beans, Phaseolus vulgaris, from the Quechua purutu; and lima beans, Phaseolus lunatus from the Quechua, pallar). Chilean green beans and cranberry beans, pototos verdes y granadosThe Jesuit, Juan Ignacio Molina (1740-1829), writing in h: ising of a book on agriculture published by his brother Francisco Antonio [Molina] de Cevallos wrote about the poroto bayo. He describes it as follows: "The Bayonese bean or common white legume known to our people, called 'porote' and also simply referred in its pure state with no other name than ‘bean’ is a plant of indisputable value for human sustenance... This seed has been introduced into the fields by man since ancient times; it grows abundantly on all lands suitable or unsuitant to cultivation, thus giving strength as well. It's also called 'poroto granado' because when ripe and dried in its pod they resemble grains of corn." Molina mentions that beans are "an essential ingredient for our diet" where the people would not be able without it. The porote bayo or common white bean is an integral part to many Chileans. It's a food with high nutrition

texts.full_text: as a group of states and state transitions and checking that a desired property holds in the model. An exhaustive search of all possible states of the model may be performed in order to verify a desired property. As the size and complexity of designs increase, much effort may be expended to improve the efficiency of automated formal verification methods. One technique used in symbolic model checking to improve efficiency is to employ binary decision diagrams (BDDs). A BDD is a directed acyclic graph that represents a Boolean expression. FIG. 1, to which reference may be now briefly made, shows an exemplary BDD for the expression ((a1 iff a2) and (b1 iff b2), where “iff” stands for “if and only if”. Each circle indicates a variable (a1, a2, b1,b2) and each box (or node) represents an AND or OR operation. The top node represents the entire Boolean expression. The paths from the top node to the bottom nodes (labeled 1 or 0) represent the different ways in which the expression can be satisfied (1) or unsatisfied (0). In symbolic model checking, BDDs are used to represent the state space of a system

texts.full_text: ck turns to 0V, the transistor stays on for almost 3 microseconds before it fully turns off and the collector settles to 5V. This can also be observed in the Base and Clock graph, where the base of the transistor is at around 0.75V for almost 3 microseconds before it starts transitioning to 0V. The Collector and Base graph helps show that the transistor is not operating in the saturation region since Vb <= Vc. Vc seems to fall below Vb for a very short time, but I do not believe this is causing saturation. In fact, a Schottky diode would not help at all since the voltages are very close to each other. Hence, this should not be a storage-time issue. I have also ruled out the transistor input capacitance (of max 20pf) to be the problem as I believe I would see similar delays in turn-on time in that case. Additionally, the RC time constant would be 0.3 microseconds (5RC = 1.5us) at the input. What could be the problem? Edit/Updated Following the accepted answer, adding a reverse diode accross the base resistor solved the issue. The updated graphs can be found in the same document, under the Solved Trace sheet. For convenience, the updated image of the Collector and Clock graph is shown below: A: The main problem is likely to be Miller capacitance (internally 10 pf or so between collector and base) and the 15 kohm base drive resistor. CR time is going to be 150 ns due to that alone and will remain delivering current into the base for several CR times while the transistor is trying to turn off. Try putting a reverse diode across the 15 kohm resistor such that when the input goes low it removes charge via the diode rather than through the sluggish 15 kohm. I'd also consider using a potential divider at the base to deliver a lower impedance signal to the base. If you clock is 3.9 volts, potential divide down this to about 1 volt with a 4k7 across base and emitter to se

texts.full_text: Sir Zeliek an invaluable asset to the Scourge's war effort, though it also brought with it its own brand of torment. For every battle fought on frost-bitten fields or within shadowy dungeons beneath Azeroth’s surface, he relived in his mind each moment leading up to their grim fate. The scent of blood and iron mingled with the acrid stench of decay as Sir Zeliek stood atop a crumbling tower overlooking Lordaeron's desolate landscape. Below him lay miles upon miles of once-thriving cities now reduced to skeletal ruins, all thanks to his people’s relentless advance under Arthas Menethil. Yet even in this place devoid of life and warmth, memories lingered like ghosts haunting the edges of consciousness: The taste of ale shared with comrades during a rare moment's respite; laughter echoing through crowded taverns filled with patrons eager for adventure or escape from their mundane lives. And then there were those he had lost—friends who fell in battle alongside him, whose faces still haunted his dreams. But it was not just the good memories that plagued Sir Zeliek’s thoughts—they included every moment of pain and sorrow too: The cries of innocents as they perished beneath relentless assault; families torn apart by war's cruel hand. Each memory served to remind him why he fought, yet also fueled a growing sense of despair. Despite his efforts at persuasion—urging others within the Scourge ranks not just to survive but truly live—the majority remained indifferent or hostile towards such notions. They had long since abandoned any semblance of humanity in favor of blind obedience and unyielding hatred for their enemies above ground. Sir Zeliek knew he could never change them, nor did he wish too—his purpose lay elsewhere now. He fought not merely to serve his master

texts.full_text: ng a Brand with No Peer and a Customer Experience That Cannot Be Replicated Online We do understand that many of the strategies we are pursuing — opening the largest specialty retail experiences in our industry while most are shrinking the size of the retail footprint or closing stores; moving from a promotional to a membership model, while others are increasing promotions, positioning their brands around price versus product; continuing to mail inspiring Source Books, while many are eliminating catalogs; and refusing to follow the herd in self-promotion on social media, instead allowing our brand to be defined by the taste, style, design and quality of the products and experiences we are creating — are all in direct conflict with conventional wisdom and the plans being pursued by many in our industry. We believe when you step back and consider: one, we are building a brand with no peer; two, we are creating a customer experience that cannot be replicated online; and three, we have total control of our brand from concept to customer, you realize what we are building is extremely rare in today’s retail landscape, and, we would argue, will also prove to be very valuable. In closing, we are deeply grateful for our people and partners whose passion and persistence bring our vision and values to life each day, as we pursue our quest to become one of the most admired brands in the world. Carpe Diem, Gary Gary Friedman Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Fiscal 2018 Outlook Net revenues in the range of $2.53 to $2.57 billion, representing growth of 5% to 7% on a comparable 52-week vs. 52-week basis. The Company’s net revenue outlook is approximately $50 million lower than its prior expectations due to its decision to delay the opening of its new York Design Gallery (now scheduled for Fall 2018) and the Company’s first Guesthouse (now scheduled for Spring 2019) as a result of the ongoing disruption from the city’s street and infrastructure construction in the Meatpacking District. texts.full_text: d MMC's Certificates snap-in for managing the certificates themselves. Here’s how you can do it: ### Export SSL Certificates 1. Export Certificates using MMC: - Open MMC (Microsoft Management Console). - Go to File > Add/Remove Snap-in. - Select Certificates and click Add. - Choose Computer Account and then select Local Computer. Click Finish and then OK. - Navigate to Certificates (Local Computer) > Personal > Certificates. - Find the SSL certificate you want to export, right-click it, and choose All Tasks > Export. - Follow the wizard to export the certificate. Make sure to include the private key if needed and protect it with a password. ### Import SSL Certificates 1. Import Certificates on Target Server: - On the target server, open MMC and add the Certificates snap-in for the local computer as described above. - Navigate to Certificates (Local Computer) > Personal > Certificates. - Right-click Certificates, select All Tasks > Import, and follow the wizard to import the exported certificate. 2. Update IIS Bindings: - Use `appcmd` to ensure the bindings are correctly set up on the target server. You can export bindings from the source server using: ```shell appcmd list site /bindings ``` - On the target server, apply these bindings by using the `appcmd` command with the exported configuration. ### Additional Tips - Ensure that the target server has the necessary permissions to use the private key of the imported certificate. - If you need to import the root CA certificate (to avoid trust issues), export it from the source server and import it on the target server using the Trusted Root Certification Authorities store in MMC. Contents 1 Politics 2 Sociology 3 Psychology 4 See also 5 References 6 Further reading Politics[edit] Smolenski (2005) examines deference in colonial Pennsylvania, to see how claims to political authority were made, justified, and accepted or rejected. He focuses on the "colonial speech economy," that is, the implicit rules that determined who was allowed to address whom and under what conditions, and describes how the qualities that inspired deference changed in the province from 1691 to 1764. The Quaker elite initially established a monopoly on political leadership based on what they believed to be their inherent civic virtue grounded in their religious and social class. By 1760, this view had been discredited and replaced with the general consensus that civic virtue was an achieved, not an inherent, attribute and that it should be determined by the display
